Title: Ecological trade-offs between jasmonic acid-dependent direct and indirect plant defences in tritrophic interactions

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Proteinase inhibitor II; herbivore-induced volatiles including (Z)-3-hexenol and TMTT
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: protein defense compound; oxylipin/volatile organic compounds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct anti-herbivore defense and indirect defense via parasitoid attraction
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive/stronger JA-dependent direct defense and constitutive indirect defense volatile release; increases PI-II and constitutive HIPVs, reduces leafminer performance, but lowers parasitism success relative to WT
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 35S::prosystemin plants constitutively accumulated higher PI-II and emitted defense-related volatiles such as (Z)-3-hexenol and TMTT. 2. These plants showed the strongest resistance to leafminers, with lower larval survival and smaller larvae. 3. Despite stronger indirect volatile signaling, parasitism success on larvae feeding on 35S::prosystemin plants was reduced relative to WT, indicating an ecological trade-off.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression line comparison; metabolite/volatile profiling; PI-II immunodiffusion assay; insect preference and performance assays; parasitoid behavior/parasitism assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leafminer infestation; exogenous jasmonic acid treatment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering stronger direct insect resistance, but deployment should consider negative effects on third-trophic-level biological control and ecological trade-offs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced parasitoid parasitism success despite stronger direct and constitutive indirect defense
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core manipulated gene represented by 35S::prosys transgene; study focuses on ecological outcomes rather than molecular dissection of downstream signaling.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Proteinase inhibitor II; herbivore-induced volatiles including (Z)-3-hexenol and TMTT
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: protein defense compound; oxylipin/volatile organic compounds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct anti-herbivore defense and indirect defense via parasitoid attraction
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JA-deficient mutant with reduced PI-II accumulation and reduced inducible volatile emission; more susceptible to leafminers and less attractive to parasitoids, but less attractive to adult flies than WT
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. spr2 plants had impaired JA-dependent direct and indirect defenses, with reduced PI-II and lower emission of inducible volatiles after leafminer attack. 2. Leafminer larvae performed better on spr2 plants than on WT plants. 3. spr2 plants were less attractive to parasitoids and also less attractive to adult leafminers than WT, indicating a trade-off in herbivore attraction.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant comparison; volatile profiling; PI-II immunodiffusion assay; insect preference and performance assays; parasitoid behavior/parasitism assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leafminer infestation; exogenous jasmonic acid treatment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates risk of weakening JA pathway: reduced defense and biocontrol attraction, although possible reduction in herbivore attraction may occur
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased herbivore susceptibility and reduced parasitoid attraction/parasitism, but reduced attraction of adult herbivores
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states spr2 is a JA biosynthesis mutant derived from loss of function of an ω-3 chloroplast fatty acid desaturase that lowers 18:3 levels.
